title: Handling 200+ file uploads in monday.com – anyone keen to test a small helper tool?
excerpt:

posts.author: Benx (ben)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone

Our team often has to attach a few hundred PDFs or design files to a board after each project hand-off, and doing it one by one is… slow. So I put together a little Batch File Uploader app for monday.com:

  • drag-and-drop a whole folder
  • match files to items if the filename equals an Item ID / a text column / or a number column
  • handles most formats (≤ 500 MB each)

It’s free while in beta. Fill a 30-second form Batch File Uploader — Beta Application

You’ll receive the install link & quick-start guide within 24 hours

A few minutes of feedback earns 3 months of the finished version on the house – no strings attached, I’m just looking for real-world input.

Questions for you:

  1. How are you dealing with bulk uploads today?
  2. Where do things clog up most – file size, matching, API limits, or something else?
  3. Would “match by filename” save your team any clicks?

Thanks for reading, and happy to chat about any upload workflows you’re wrestling with.

— Ben @ Toolution (independent dev, not affiliated with monday.com)

posts.author: Benx (ben)
posts.cookedHtml:
Our first demo is here https://youtu.be/80wgN-YSGeA
Hope you like it.
